content.text:
```mdl
@ CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON‑DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (“CNDA”)
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_10_nda_5

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Article 2 – DEFINITIONS
type Party =
    | DisclosingParty(unit)
    | ReceivingParty(unit)

type ConfidentialInfo = {
    content: string,
    is_confidential: bool
}

type Representative = {
    is_employee: bool,          -- true if the person is an employee of the Receiving Party
    need_to_know: bool,         -- true if the person has a legitimate need to know the information
    is_rep: bool               -- true if the person is a “Representative” as defined in the CNDA
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities (public model objects)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties
entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

@ Confidential information that may be exchanged
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ Person who may receive the information on behalf of the Receiving Party
entity representative: Representative

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
func can_disclose(rep: Representative) -> bool:
    -- The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to a Representative
    -- only when that Representative is an employee and has a need‑to‑know.
    rep.is_employee and rep.need_to_know and rep.is_rep

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – normative requirements (deontic modalities)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Article 2.03 – Kept in Confidence
rule O use_reasonable_care:
    -- The Receiving Party must use at least a reasonable degree of care.
    -- This is a placeholder that is always satisfied; concrete care‑level
    -- modelling can be added later if needed.
    true always

@ Article 2.04 – No Benefit, Restricted Use
rule F derive_benefit:
    -- The Receiving Party must not derive any direct or indirect benefit from
    -- the Confidential Information.
    -- A concrete benefit flag is not modelled; the rule is expressed as a
    -- prohibition of a hypothetical “benefit_derived” predicate.
    false always

@ Article 2.05 – No Disclosure
rule P disclose_to_employee:
    -- Permission: the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information
    -- to a Representative who is an employee and has a need‑to‑know.
    can_disclose(representative) always

rule F disclose_to_non_employee:
    -- Prohibition: the Receiving Party may not disclose Confidential Information
    -- to any person who is not an employee or does not have a need‑to‑know.
    not can_disclose(representative) always

@ Article 2.06 – Disclosure Required by Law
rule P disclose_by_legal_process:
    -- Permission to disclose when required by law, provided the Receiving Party
    -- not the Disclosing Party in writing before disclosure.
    -- The “legal_process_required” flag is not modelled; the rule is a
    -- placeholder that can be instantiated by an external fact.
    true always

@ Article 2.07 – Return of Confidential Information
rule O return_on_request:
    -- Upon written request, the Receiving Party must return or destroy all
    -- Confidential Information and certify the destruction.
    true always

@ Article 2.08 – Responsibility for Others
rule O responsibility_for_reps:
    -- The Receiving Party is responsible for any breach caused by its
    -- Representatives.
    true always

@ Article 3.01 – Remedies (specific performance)
rule O specific_performance:
    -- The Disclosing Party may seek specific performance for any breach.
    true always

@ Article 3.02 – Continuing Obligations
rule O obligations_survive:
    -- Confidentiality obligations survive termination of the underlying contract.
    true always

@ Article 3.09 – Governing Law
rule O governing_law:
    -- The agreement is governed by the laws of the Province of Alberta.
    true always

@ Article 3.11 – Residual Information
rule P use_residual_information:
    -- The Receiving Party may use residual information (knowledge retained
    -- in the unaided memory of a skilled person) after the term.
    true always
```
